paper_title: Paleoecological and Taphonomic Analysis of Pleistocene Hominin Sites

methods.description: This method involves analyzing bone assemblages to identify signs of anthropic breakage and tooth marks, which help infer the interactions between hominins and carnivores, and the sequence of access to carcasses.

steps.description: Document the presence of diagnostic criteria such as percussion pits to attribute breakage to hominins.
steps.searchable_summary: identify percussion pits to determine hominin activity

steps.description: Analyze the association of tooth marks and anthropic breakage to propose secondary access of human groups to carcasses.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze tooth marks and breakage for access sequence

steps.description: Consider the preservation state of bone surfaces to assess the potential masking of human intervention evidence.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate bone preservation for evidence masking

methods.description: This method uses anatomical profiles to reconstruct paleoecological environments and infer levels of competition and resource availability among Pleistocene hominins and carnivores.

steps.description: Use anatomical profiles to infer levels of ravaging by bone-crushing carnivores.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze anatomical profiles for carnivore ravaging

steps.description: Correlate ravaging levels with competition in the environment.
Consider the presence of hominin populations and their impact on resource availability and competition.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze hominin impact on resources and competition
